_meta.description: Configuration for automated re-analysis trigger checks. Controls which analyses are monitored, which data sources are critical vs informational, and how notifications are thresholded.

mn-poverty-change.label: Minnesota Poverty Change 2017–2022
mn-poverty-change.notes: ACS multi-vintage analysis — flag immediately when new ACS 5-year vintage drops

ks-poverty-health.label: Kansas Poverty & Health Access
ks-poverty-health.notes: Uses ACS + CDC PLACES + HRSA HPSAs — three active annual/quarterly sources

ks-healthcare-access.label: Kansas Healthcare Access
ks-healthcare-access.notes: HRSA HPSAs update quarterly; flag on major designation changes

la-environmental-justice.label: Los Angeles Environmental Justice
la-environmental-justice.notes: EPA EJScreen updates annually in late summer

chicago-food-access.label: Chicago Food Access
chicago-food-access.notes: USDA SNAP + ACS — flag when ACS vintage changes

tx-healthcare-access.label: Texas Healthcare Access
tx-healthcare-access.notes: Large state — re-run cost is high, only flag CRITICAL or HIGH severity

ks-tracts-demo.label: Kansas Census Tracts Baseline
ks-tracts-demo.notes: Baseline geometry only; no analytical sources to track

acs_5year.notes: Primary social/economic data source for most analyses. A new vintage means new population, poverty, and income estimates — always triggers re-run review.

tiger_tracts.notes: Tract geometries. Changes rarely affect interpretations, but if a geographic boundary revision occurs, re-run is required.

tiger_county.notes: County boundaries used for reference/overlay. Minor updates rarely affect analysis.

tiger_state.notes: State boundary used for outline only. No re-run trigger.

cdc_places.notes: Health measures at the tract level. Annual release. New vintage replaces all health outcome estimates.

hrsa_hpsas.notes: Shortage area designations change quarterly. Any new designation file may affect healthcare access scores.

ejscreen.notes: EPA EJScreen updates annually. Score changes can shift environmental justice findings.

usda_snap.notes: SNAP retailer locations change continuously. Re-run when data is >6 months old.

fcc_broadband.notes: Broadband availability used as secondary infrastructure indicator.

epa_superfund.notes: Superfund sites rarely change. Re-run only on major list revision.

hud_lihtc.notes: LIHTC properties used as housing resource overlay. Annual update.

dot_lodes.notes: Employment origin-destination data. Used in commute/labor market analyses only.

notification_thresholds.description: Controls what level of update triggers a notification or rerun flag.

levels.none: Never flag this source for re-run triggering
levels.major: Only flag when 2+ vintages behind or a major boundary/schema change
levels.minor: Flag on any newer vintage, even 1 year behind

impact_scoring.description: Weights used by check_reanalysis_needed.py to compute impact scores (0.0–1.0).

schedule.description: When to run automatic re-analysis checks.
schedule.notes: Run on the 1st of each month at 8am. Catches ACS (December), CDC PLACES (May), and quarterly HRSA updates.
